services.title: Lawn Irrigation
services.tagline: Custom-designed sprinkler systems engineered for your lawn's specific zones, slopes, and soil. Installation, maintenance, repair, and seasonal service.
services.shortDescription: Custom sprinkler systems designed, installed, and maintained for Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin lawns.
services.metaTitle: Lawn Irrigation & Sprinkler Systems in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin | Allscape Services
services.metaDescription: Professional sprinkler system design, installation, and maintenance. Custom irrigation built for Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin soil and climate. Free quotes since 2000.
services.h1: Lawn Irrigation in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin

subheadings.title: Sprinkler System Installation
subheadings.body: Every yard is different. We custom-design irrigation systems with the right zones, head types, and pressure for your lawn's specific layout, slopes, and soil conditions. Our installs are clean, properly buried, and built to last.

subheadings.title: Maintenance & Repair
subheadings.body: Whether your existing system needs a tune-up, a broken head replaced, or zone troubleshooting, we can help. Annual maintenance plans available.

subheadings.title: Spring Start-Up & Winterization
subheadings.body: Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin winters are hard on irrigation. We handle proper winterization in fall and full system start-up and inspection in spring.

subheadings.title: RPZ Backflow Testing
subheadings.body: Required annually in Illinois on any property with an irrigation system. RPZ testing must be performed by a licensed plumber with a CCDI license — we don't do it in-house. We keep an updated list of plumbers we've worked with, organized by city.

faqs.question: How much does a sprinkler system cost in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on yard size, head count, and system complexity. Call for pricing or to schedule a free on-site quote.

faqs.question: How long does installation take?
faqs.answer: Most residential installs take 1–2 days. We coordinate with utility locates and work efficiently to minimize disruption to your yard.

faqs.question: Do you service systems you didn't install?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can troubleshoot, repair, or upgrade existing systems regardless of who installed them.

faqs.question: When should I schedule winterization?
faqs.answer: We recommend booking by late September to ensure your system is winterized before the first hard freeze in mid-to-late October.

services.title: Landscape Lighting
services.tagline: Professional path lighting, uplighting, and architectural illumination. Designed to extend your home's curb appeal long after sunset.
services.shortDescription: Path lighting, uplighting, wash lights — designed and installed to bring your yard to life after dark.
services.metaTitle: Landscape Lighting Installation in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin | Allscape Services
services.metaDescription: Professional landscape lighting design and installation. Path lights, uplighting, architectural illumination. Serving Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin since 2000.
services.h1: Landscape Lighting in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin

subheadings.title: Path & Walkway Lighting
subheadings.body: Safe, beautiful illumination for walkways, garden paths, and entryways. Low-voltage LED systems that look great and run efficiently year-round.

subheadings.title: Architectural Uplighting
subheadings.body: Highlight the best features of your home — facades, columns, mature trees, and key architectural elements. Designed to flatter your house, not flood it.

subheadings.title: Garden & Feature Lighting
subheadings.body: Wash lights for plantings, downlights from trees for moonlight effects, accent lighting for water features and focal points.

subheadings.title: Maintenance & Upgrades
subheadings.body: We service existing landscape lighting systems — bulb replacements, transformer issues, fixture upgrades from halogen to LED, and timer/photocell tuning.

faqs.question: Are your systems LED?
faqs.answer: Yes. All new installs use low-voltage LED fixtures — they last longer, run cooler, use a fraction of the power, and look better than halogen.

faqs.question: How much does landscape lighting cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the number of fixtures, the design complexity, and the property layout. We provide free in-person consultations.

faqs.question: Will it work in winter?
faqs.answer: Yes. LED systems function in any temperature. We bury wiring at proper depth so freezing isn't an issue.

services.title: Holiday Lighting
services.tagline: Seasonal Christmas lighting installation and removal. Permanent year-round programmable systems available — Inception certified installer.
services.shortDescription: Seasonal install, takedown, and storage. Permanent year-round systems available.
services.metaTitle: Holiday & Christmas Lighting Installation in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin | Allscape Services
services.metaDescription: Professional holiday lighting installation, takedown, and storage. Plus permanent year-round programmable systems. Inception certified installer in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin.
services.h1: Holiday Lighting in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin

subheadings.title: Seasonal Christmas Lighting
subheadings.body: Our flagship holiday service. We design, install, take down, and store your Christmas lighting display each year. Roof lines, trees, wreaths, garlands, and yard features. We supply all the lights and materials — commercial-grade only, built to handle Northern Illinois winters.

subheadings.title: Annual Takedown & Storage
subheadings.body: After the holidays, we return to remove every light, wrap and label them properly, and store them in our facility for next year. No tangles, no climbing, no dragging boxes from the attic.

subheadings.title: Permanent Year-Round Lighting
subheadings.body: For homeowners who want lighting that stays up year-round and changes with the seasons. App-controlled, programmable color and pattern, hidden under eaves or blended into gutter lines and fascia. We are a certified Inception installer.

faqs.question: When should I book seasonal Christmas lighting?
faqs.answer: Earlier is better — our calendar fills by October. We start installs in early November and run through early December.

faqs.question: What's the difference between seasonal and permanent lighting?
faqs.answer: Seasonal lighting is installed in November and removed in January. Permanent lighting stays up year-round, hidden under eaves or blended into gutter lines and fascia, and is controlled by an app for any color, pattern, or holiday.

faqs.question: Do you install retail brands like Govee or Twinkly?
faqs.answer: No. The only permanent system we install is Inception — a professional, permanent-mount system built for outdoor durability with full warranty support. We don't install retail/DIY brands.

faqs.question: Do you store the lights between seasons?
faqs.answer: Yes. After takedown, we wrap, label, and store everything at our facility so it's ready for next year's install — no clutter in your garage.

services.title: Paver Restoration
services.tagline: Professional cleaning, polymeric re-sanding, sealing, and re-leveling of brick paver patios, walkways, and driveways. Bring tired pavers back to life.
services.shortDescription: Cleaning, re-leveling, polymeric re-sanding, and sealing — bringing tired pavers back to life.
services.metaTitle: Brick Paver Cleaning, Sealing & Restoration in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin | Allscape Services
services.metaDescription: Professional brick paver restoration in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin. Cleaning, re-leveling, polymeric re-sanding, and sealing for patios, walkways, and driveways.
services.h1: Brick Paver Cleaning, Sealing & Restoration in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin

subheadings.title: Power Washing & Deep Cleaning
subheadings.body: Years of grime, mildew, moss, and weeds disappear with our professional cleaning process. We remove stains and old joint sand, leaving your pavers ready for proper restoration.

subheadings.title: Re-Leveling
subheadings.body: Sunken or shifted pavers? We carefully lift, re-base, and re-set them so your patio or walkway is flat, stable, and safe to walk on.

subheadings.title: Polymeric Re-Sanding
subheadings.body: We replace failed joint sand with high-grade polymeric sand. Once activated, it locks pavers in place, stops weed growth, and prevents wash-out.

subheadings.title: Sealing
subheadings.body: Premium sealers protect your investment from UV fade, stains, and freeze-thaw damage. Matte and wet-look finishes available to enhance natural color depth.

services.important_note: We do paver restoration, not new paver installation. If your existing patio, walkway, or driveway needs to be cleaned, leveled, re-sanded, or sealed — we're your team. For new paver installations, we can refer you to a trusted partner.

faqs.question: How often should pavers be cleaned and sealed?
faqs.answer: In the Chicago/Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin climate, every 3–5 years is typical. Our freeze-thaw cycles and road salt are hard on paver joints, so regular maintenance is the difference between pavers that last 20+ years and ones that fail in 10.

faqs.question: Do you install new pavers?
faqs.answer: No. We focus on restoration only — cleaning, re-leveling, polymeric re-sanding, and sealing of existing brick paver surfaces. For new installs, we can refer you to a trusted local partner.

faqs.question: Will sealing make my pavers slippery?
faqs.answer: No. The sealers we use are formulated for outdoor traffic and don't reduce traction in wet conditions.

faqs.question: How much does paver restoration cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on square footage, condition, and which steps are needed (cleaning, re-leveling, polymeric re-sanding, sealing). We provide free on-site quotes.

services.title: Drainage
services.tagline: Yard drainage solutions for properties dealing with low spots, soggy lawns, downspout overflow, or persistent surface water.
services.shortDescription: Yard drainage solutions for Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin properties.
services.metaTitle: Yard Drainage in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin | Allscape Services
services.metaDescription: Yard drainage solutions for low spots, soggy lawns, downspout overflow, and surface runoff. Serving Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin homeowners since 2000.
services.h1: Yard Drainage in Northern Illinois & Southern Wisconsin
services.important_note: We do French drains, surface drainage, downspout work, catch basins, rain gardens, and rock gardens. We do not regrade and we do not do foundation work. For projects that require major earth movement, grade changes, or foundation waterproofing, we'd recommend bringing in a contractor who specializes in that work.

subheadings.title: French Drains
subheadings.body: French drains move water underground via perforated pipe surrounded by gravel. They're the standard solution for chronically saturated soil and persistent low spots that can't be addressed at the surface.

subheadings.title: Surface Drainage
subheadings.body: Surface drainage uses swales, channels, and surface inlets to move standing water off the yard along the existing grade. It works when there's a clear path the water can follow once the route is established.

subheadings.title: Downspout Work
subheadings.body: Downspouts dumping water close to the house are a common cause of saturated beds, soggy lawn areas, and overflow during heavy rain. Extensions, buried piping to pop-up emitters, or routed runs to a discharge point move that water to where it belongs.

subheadings.title: Catch Basins
subheadings.body: Catch basins are surface inlets with a grate top and a piped outlet. They're useful where surface water collects in a low point — driveway aprons, patios, and yard depressions — and needs to be routed somewhere it won't pool.

subheadings.title: Rain Gardens
subheadings.body: Rain gardens are shallow planted depressions designed to absorb and slowly infiltrate runoff. They handle roof and driveway runoff while doubling as a designed planting bed, and they reduce the load on traditional drainage routes.

subheadings.title: Rock Gardens
subheadings.body: Dry-creek-style rock gardens combine drainage function with landscape aesthetics. They route surface water along a stone channel while integrating into the planting design — a good choice where a rain garden isn't suitable.

faqs.question: Why does my yard have standing water after rain?
faqs.answer: Common causes are soil compaction, low spots in the existing grade, downspouts dumping water too close to the house, or clay layers below the surface that slow infiltration. The right fix depends on what's driving it — French drains, downspout extensions, surface inlets, and rain gardens each address different causes.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a French drain and surface drainage?
faqs.answer: French drains move water underground via perforated pipe and gravel. Surface drainage moves water across the ground via swales and surface inlets. Each suits a different scenario — the right choice depends on soil type, slope, and where the water needs to discharge.

faqs.question: What kinds of drainage work don't you do?
faqs.answer: We don't regrade and we don't do foundation work. We focus on French drains, surface drainage, downspout work, catch basins, rain gardens, and rock gardens. For regrading, major earth movement, or foundation waterproofing, we'd recommend a contractor who specializes in that work.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year for drainage work?
faqs.answer: Most drainage installations happen between mid-spring (after the ground thaws and dries) and late fall (before hard freeze). A visit right after heavy rain can actually help diagnose the problem — it's easier to see where water collects and which way it flows.

faqs.question: Will I need a permit?
faqs.answer: It varies by municipality. Larger grading projects, modifications to stormwater systems, or work in regulated wetlands often require permits. Smaller-scale installations like buried French drains or downspout extensions usually don't. We'll flag it before work starts if a permit applies.
